summary: This initiative details dimensional standards for accessory apartments and two-family housing units in Chapel Hill. For accessory apartments, regulations include a maximum floor area of 1,000 sq ft (with potential exceptions), a height limit of 29 feet, an impervious surface ratio of 0.50, a density of one accessory apartment per zoning lot, occupancy limits of up to 4 unrelated people, and a maximum of 1 parking space per bedroom. For two-family units, standards include a maximum floor area of 5,000 sq ft, heights determined by the zoning district, an impervious surface ratio of 0.50, occupancy limits of up to 4 unrelated people per unit, no minimum parking requirement but a maximum of 4 outdoor spaces on-site, and 40% tree canopy coverage. Both housing types require a minimum lot size as specified by the zoning district.
